module_title: Stressors Affecting Thought Processes and Perceptions
learning_objectives: Differentiate between schizophrenia and other psychotic disorders.
Identify and manage positive, negative, and cognitive symptoms of schizophrenia.
Implement effective interventions for acute psychotic episodes.

sections.title: Introduction and Overview
sections.content: Psychotic disorders like schizophrenia involve a break with reality. This section introduces key diagnoses and the hallmark symptoms that define them.

sections.title: Positive Symptoms
sections.content: Delusions, hallucinations, and disorganized behavior are additions to normal experience. Recognizing these signs helps nurses ensure safety and respond therapeutically.

sections.title: Negative and Cognitive Symptoms
sections.content: Blunted affect, avolition, memory problems, and poor concentration reduce functioning and often persist beyond acute episodes.

sections.title: Neurobiological Insights
sections.content: Dopamine dysregulation and other neurotransmitter changes underlie many psychotic symptoms. Antipsychotic medications target these pathways.

sections.title: Medication Basics
sections.content: Typical and atypical antipsychotics reduce positive symptoms but carry side effects like EPS or metabolic changes. Monitoring and education promote adherence.

sections.title: Nursing Care During Acute Psychosis
sections.content: During hallucinations or severe delusions, maintain calm communication, reduce stimulation, and ensure the patient and others remain safe.

sections.title: Long-Term Management
sections.content: After stabilization, supportive therapy, community programs, and medication help prevent relapse and foster recovery.

sections.title: Family Education and Support
sections.content: Families benefit from learning about early warning signs, medication management, and ways to encourage treatment engagement.

sections.title: Legal and Ethical Considerations
sections.content: Involuntary treatment laws protect patients and the public when psychosis impairs judgment. Nurses uphold rights while advocating for least restrictive care.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: Understanding psychotic disorders equips nurses to intervene compassionately and effectively across care settings.
